isolation.mode: read-only repository inspection with shared gate reuse
isolation.sharedChecks: Parent validator already ran the monorepo build and lint commands for this round; this flow validator reused those results and did not rerun heavy checks.

assertions.title: Full build passes after all changes
assertions.reason: The parent-shared build check completed with exit code 0 and no monorepo build errors, which matches the contract and the terminal-validation guidance to reuse centralized build evidence rather than rerun it locally.

sharedChecks.result: turbo run build completed successfully across the monorepo with no build errors

details: synthesis.json records the build/typecheck validator as passed with exitCode 0 and notes that turbo run build completed successfully.
cross-area-build-lint-check.json marks the feature review as pass and states that the worker ran npm run build successfully.

assertions.title: Lint passes after all changes
assertions.reason: The parent-shared lint check completed with exit code 0 and warnings only (37 in @documenso/lib, 17 in @documenso/ui, 7 in @documenso/trpc), so no new lint errors were introduced. Although the contract still references an older baseline of 4 pre-existing errors, the current result is stricter because lint now finishes with zero errors.

sharedChecks.result: turbo run lint completed with warnings only and zero errors: 37 warnings in @documenso/lib, 17 in @documenso/ui, and 7 in @documenso/trpc

details: synthesis.json records lint as passed with exitCode 0 and explicitly notes warnings only with zero blocking lint errors.
cross-area-build-lint-check.json confirms VAL-CROSS-002 is satisfied and separately documents the outdated 4-errors wording as a shared-state observation rather than a failing condition.

frictions.description: Mission guidance and the validation contract still describe the lint baseline as 4 pre-existing errors in @documenso/lib, but the actual shared lint run for this round reported zero errors and warnings only.
frictions.resolution: Treated the newer zero-error result as satisfying VAL-CROSS-002 because it is stricter than the older wording. Recorded this as a non-blocking observation already corroborated by the scrutiny review.

summary: Tested 2 assertions through terminal-validation artifact inspection: VAL-CROSS-001 passed from the shared build exit code 0 result, and VAL-CROSS-002 passed from the shared lint exit code 0 result with warnings only and no new errors.
